title: escalation_resolved event payload
$comment: Provenance: authored 2026-04-23 in WU 3.4 as the Phase 3 absorption of Phase 2 carry-item F2.10 (no machine-readable signal that a prior `human_escalation` has been resolved; inbox file orphaning). Substrate event — usable by any role, discriminated by `resolution_kind`. At v1 the enum covers `qa_regression_resolved` (emitted by the qa-regression skill when a QA repeat-failure escalation is closed by a subsequent `qa_execution_completed`) and `human_resolved` (emitted by the human, or by any role on the human's behalf, to retire an escalation whose underlying problem was resolved out of band — e.g., the plan-review cycle from FEAT-2026-0005 Edit C-fix). First application: retires `inbox/human-escalation/FEAT-2026-0005-plan-review-cycle.md` in this WU's commit by appending a `human_resolved` instance to `events/FEAT-2026-0005.jsonl`.
description: Payload shape for the `escalation_resolved` event appended to the event log when a prior `human_escalation` has been resolved and its inbox artifact should be considered retired. The envelope `correlation_id` of this event MUST equal the `correlation_id` of the `human_escalation` event it resolves — feature-level (FEAT-YYYY-NNNN) if the original escalation was feature-level, task-level (FEAT-YYYY-NNNN/TNN) if the original escalation was task-level. The `resolution_kind` field is the discriminator: consumers dispatch on it to route the resolution to the correct downstream handler (regression-suite curation for `qa_regression_resolved`, inbox archival for `human_resolved`).

resolution_kind.description: Closed enum discriminating how the escalation was resolved. `qa_regression_resolved` is emitted by the qa-regression skill when a subsequent `qa_execution_completed` post-dates a `human_escalation` filed via the repeat-failure path. `human_resolved` is emitted by the human (source: `human`) or by any role on the human's behalf to retire an escalation whose underlying blocker was resolved out of band — e.g., a plan-review cycle unstuck by a plan-file edit, a spec-level blocker closed by a spec change. New resolution kinds land additively as future roles introduce resolution semantics (e.g., `override_reauthorized` for override-expiry flows).

resolved_escalation_event_ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the `human_escalation` event this resolution retires. Backlink into the event log; paired with the envelope `correlation_id` (which matches the original escalation's correlation_id) a consumer can locate the original escalation without scanning.

resolved_escalation_inbox_file.description: Optional path to the `human_escalation` inbox file being retired, relative to the orchestration repo root (e.g. `inbox/human-escalation/FEAT-2026-0005-plan-review-cycle.md`). Mirrors `human_escalation.inbox_file` from the resolved escalation's payload. Included when the original escalation recorded an `inbox_file` field; omitted otherwise. Recorded here so the resolution event and the physical file retirement are threaded through the same record.

summary.description: One-sentence prose description of how the escalation was resolved. Mirrors the shape of `human_escalation.summary`. Must be sufficient for a cold reader to understand what unblocked the feature or task — lead with the concrete change (e.g., "cycle removed by reverting T01 depends_on to []"), not the outcome label.
